metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I can't figure out why I'm so bothered
there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
age is an issue for others but not for me
behaviors: going home excited to see him

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
It sounds like you're really trying to make sense of all these different perspectives on your relationship. On one hand, you feel incredibly lucky and happy in this connection, but on the other, you're carrying around these worries about what others might think. What do you think might be behind those thoughts about age?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... it’s hard not to think about it, you know? Sometimes I feel like people are judging us, and I guess I worry that they’ll see it as some kind of joke or something that makes me look foolish. I really do love him, but then these thoughts creep in, and it just messes with my head. I don’t want to feel embarrassed about something that's so important to me.

automatic_thoughts: they'll see it as a joke
it makes me look foolish
I don't want to feel embarrassed

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear how difficult it must be to manage those intrusive thoughts, especially when you're worried about others' perceptions. It sounds like the fear of being judged or appearing foolish is creating a lot of anxiety for you. Can you tell me more about what makes these thoughts particularly challenging to manage?

patient.query: Yeah, it just feels really overwhelming sometimes. Like, there’s this voice in my head that keeps reminding me of all the times I’ve been criticized or laughed at. It makes me second-guess everything in my relationship. I want to be open and happy, but I get so caught up in worrying about how I’m seen that it just paralyzes me. It’s exhausting, honestly.

automatic_thoughts: I second-guess everything in my relationship
I can't be open and happy
I'm being judged

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the fear of being judged is really weighing on you, and that internal pressure must feel incredibly draining. When you say it makes you feel paralyzed, what does that feel like in your body?

patient.query: Yeah, it really does drain me. When I say paralyzed, it’s like my chest gets tight, and I can’t really breathe right. My stomach flips, and I just feel this heaviness that makes it hard to think straight. It's like I want to run away from the situation, but I also want to stay and be present. It’s confusing, and I end up just... avoiding things instead.

automatic_thoughts: I can't really breathe right
I want to run away from the situation
I want to stay and be present
It's confusing

neutral: Fear of awareness under general anesthetic (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like what you're describing is really heavy and overwhelming. The physical sensations and the confusion you're experiencing must be difficult to navigate. Can you tell me a bit more about how these feelings impact your day-to-day life?

patient.query: It really does affect so much of my day-to-day. Some days I just feel like I can’t even leave the house. I’ll cancel plans with friends or bail on things I thought I’d enjoy because I can’t shake that tight feeling in my chest. And then I feel guilty for letting people down, which just adds to the whole cycle of anxiety. It feels like I’m missing out, but at the same time, I don’t know how to face it all.

automatic_thoughts: I can’t even leave the house
I’ll let people down
I’m missing out
I don’t know how to face it all

neutral: Anxiety about having a heart attack (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like anxiety is really impacting your ability to engage in everyday activities. The tight feeling in your chest and the guilt you feel about canceling plans must be incredibly draining. How does that sense of being stuck feel in your body?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s really rough. It’s like there’s this knot in my stomach, and I can feel my heart racing. It’s uncomfortable and just... heavy. I guess I feel trapped sometimes, like I’m watching life happen around me but can’t participate. I want to be part of it, but my body is just screaming at me to stay put. It’s frustrating.

automatic_thoughts: I feel trapped
I can't participate
My body is screaming to stay put

llm_response.response: "It sounds like your body is carrying a lot right now, with that tightness and racing feeling. When you say you feel trapped, it must be really frustrating to feel disconnected from what's happening around you. Can you tell me more about that?"

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely feels like my body is at war with my mind or something. I’ll be in a room full of friends, but it feels like there’s this invisible wall between us. I want to connect and laugh, but then everything just feels out of reach. I often think, "Why can’t I just be normal and chill like everyone else?" It’s just really isolating, you know?

triggers: being in a room full of friends
automatic_thoughts: Why can’t I just be normal and chill like everyone else?

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: I hear you feeling caught between wanting to connect and struggling to do so, like there's an invisible barrier. It sounds really painful to be surrounded by people yet feel so disconnected.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. Sometimes I catch myself smiling and trying to join in, but then this wave of self-doubt hits me. I start thinking, "What if they see through my smile and realize I’m not really here?" It's like I’m putting on a show when all I want is to be genuinely with them. That just adds to the loneliness, even when I’m not alone.

automatic_thoughts: What if they see through my smile?
I’m not really here
I’m putting on a show

llm_response.response: That sounds really difficult to navigate. It can feel exhausting to try so hard to connect while still feeling unseen. Maybe you could reflect on whether there are moments when you feel more genuinely present, even if it's just for a brief moment?

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel exhausting, and I guess there are little moments here and there where I do feel a bit more connected, like when someone really gets me or when we share a good laugh. But they feel fleeting, like they slip away just as quickly as they come. It’s hard to hold onto those moments, especially when I’m in my head. I wonder if I could find a way to capture that feeling longer... but then I spiral back into worry about how to do that.

automatic_thoughts: they feel fleeting
I wonder if I could find a way to capture that feeling longer
I spiral back into worry

llm_response.response: It sounds like those brief moments of connection provide some relief from the exhaustion you're feeling, but they don't last long enough to make a big difference. When those moments slip away, what happens? And how does that worry about capturing them feel in your body?

patient.query: Yeah, when those moments slip away, it’s like this sudden drop in my chest, almost like a wave crashing down. I feel this sadness and frustration, like I’m back in that lonely space again. It’s disheartening because I want to feel good and light, but instead, I feel that heaviness creeping back in. And when I think about trying to hold onto those moments, it just makes my stomach twist again, like I'm putting pressure on myself to recreate something that was just

triggers: thinking about trying to hold onto moments
automatic_thoughts: I want to feel good and light
I'm putting pressure on myself to recreate something

llm_response.response: "It sounds like those moments slipping away really hit you hard, creating a physical weight that brings back that heavy feeling. The frustration and sadness you're describing must be really difficult to carry around, especially when you're trying to hold onto something that just slips through your fingers."
